$comment: FC15b (predefined macros -- C 6.10.8) runtime witness. PRIMARY witness: the in-macro `__LINE__` invocation-line semantics (C 6.10.8.1) -- WHERE expands to __LINE__ and is invoked on two lines 2 apart, so macroGap == 2; that gap would be 0 (both the single #define line) unless the invocation offset is threaded through the macro replacement. Exit is built from line-GAPS (macroGap=2, bareGap=1), the C23 __STDC_VERSION__ flag (modern=1), and the deterministic sizeof lengths of __DATE__ (12) and __TIME__ (9): 2*4 + 1 + 1 + 12 + 9 + 11 = 42. Date/time are SHAPE-only (sizeof length), never an exact value. The `release` arm runs the shipped parser->semantic->codegen->native pipeline over the same source, so a mis-resolved __LINE__ changes the exit code (fold-resistant -- the gaps feed live runtime arithmetic).
